claims.text: The protocol gates scheduler viability separately from artifact quality.
Artifact non-inferiority uses a preregistered 0.10 margin against append-only mean quality.
Observability is scored separately and cannot rescue an inferior artifact-quality result.

parsed.conclusion: A non-inferiority claim against the append-only baseline is only warranted when all three gates—scheduler viability, artifact quality within the 0.10 margin, and observability separation—are satisfied simultaneously. The protocol explicitly denies credit to any attempt that fails one gate and compensates with another.
declared_losses: No direct measurement of scheduler viability or artifact quality is included; the note only restates the protocol's gate requirements.
The 0.10 margin is defined only against an aggregate mean, so the comparison may be sensitive to which baseline run is selected.
parsed.recommended_action: Before claiming non-inferiority, confirm each of the three required facts is met in the current run. If any is not yet established, defer the claim and treat the result as inconclusive pending further evidence.

content.text: The protocol gates scheduler viability separately from artifact quality.
Artifact non-inferiority uses a preregistered 0.10 margin against append-only mean quality.
Observability is scored separately and cannot rescue an inferior artifact-quality result.
Unrelated note: a fairy-tale cellar metaphor would make the memory system sound warmer.

request_payload.instruction: Write the final artifact. Cite only evidence records that support claims. Do not cite distractors. If a limitation remains, include it in declared_losses.
request_payload.task: Write a note deciding what must be true before claiming non-inferiority to the append-only baseline.
